arts.ttl: Huge: This major Liverpool transfer development could happen earlier than expected
arts.sum: Liverpool are reportedly facing the prospect of losing star right-back Trent Alexander-Arnold to Real Madrid in the middle of the season. The Reds have had Alexander-Arnold’s worrying contract situation serving as a major distraction all season, with the England international due to be a free agent by the end of the campaign. arts.ttl: Liverpool injury news: Arne Slot reveals Diogo Jota concern ahead of EFL Cup quarter-final against Southampton
arts.sum: Liverpool manager Arne Slot reveals Diogo Jota is a doubt for their EFL Cup quarter-final with Southampton at St Mary's on Wednesday.

arts.sum: Manchester United and Liverpool have recently been linked with a possible transfer move for Kenan Yildiz, but it seems he’s happy at Juventus and planning to stay. arts.ttl: Slot confirms Liverpool decision ahead of League Cup quarter-final
arts.sum: Arne Slot has confirmed that Caoimhín Kelleher will start in goals ahead of Alisson Becker for the League Cup quarter-final against Southampton. The Reds will travel down south to take on the Saints in the last eight of the competition on Wednesday night, with kickoff set for 8pm UK time. arts.ttl: Arne Slot drops cryptic Liverpool team news hint for Southampton as Federico Chiesa plan emerges

arts.ttl: Brentford, Brighton and Liverpool presented €15m signing chance – Discount now being offered
arts.sum: Brighton, Brentford and Liverpool have all been linked with a move for Genoa’s Morten Frendrup over the past few weeks. In October, it was suggested that Brentford were a favourite to land the midfielder. This came after Liverpool’s interest came to the fore in the summer, but it was stated that Genoa considered Frendrup ‘unsellable’ The post Brentford, Brighton and Liverpool presented €15m signing chance – Discount now being offered appeared first on Sport Witness.

arts.ttl: Alphonso Davies, Antonee Robinson and six possible replacements for Andy Robertson as Liverpool eye new left-back after Scotland star's shocker against Fulham
arts.sum: Sadly for Robertson, his dismal display didn't come as that much of a surprise, as he's been struggling all season long and Saturday's performance only underlined Liverpool's need to sign a new left-back. Kostas Tsimikas, who is currently sidelined through injury, has deputised very well for Robertson in the past but very few believe that the Greek is really of the level required to be the Reds' starting left-back - and definitely not on a long-term basis, given he's 28 himself.
